codeReview.summary: The assigned fix session did not introduce code/test changes for DashboardQuickSwitchView contracts, and prior blocking assertion gaps remain in the current test suite. While production switch-failure announcements now exist, view-level assertion-backed coverage for required error/action wiring and failure-path announcements is still incomplete.

issues.description: Retry control presence is still weakly asserted: `test_errorState_hasRetryButton` uses `sut.find(ViewType.Button.self)` and can pass on a non-error-state button (e.g., header settings), so it does not contractually prove Retry is rendered in the error branch.

Retry action wiring is still not assertion-backed: `test_errorState_retryButtonTriggersReload` taps `buttons.first` without proving it is the Retry control and without asserting post-tap state/announcement changes.

Launch announcement test remains permissive (`launched successfully` OR `Launch error`) and is satisfied by DEBUG stub behavior rather than production launch semantics, so required success/failure announcement verification is not strict.
DEBUG hook `testTriggerLaunch(profile:)` always emits `Launch error: Test launch path ...`, preventing assertion-backed validation of real launch success/failure announcement paths.
Load-error announcement verification is still generic (`announcement.contains("loaded") || announcement.contains("Error")`) and does not induce/confirm the explicit error-transition contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker marked `followedProcedure: true` without required broad app test/manual UI evidence and without context-read evidence required by the skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requires `scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh` and manual checks (.factory/skills/switcher-ui-worker/SKILL.md:32-33) plus context-read evidence when `followedProcedure=true` (.factory/skills/switcher-ui-worker/SKILL.md:96-112). Handoff shows only `commandsRun` entries and `interactiveChecks: []` with `followedProcedure: true` (/Users/dewclaw/.factory/missions/11939f59-0807-4ae2-ae9a-d828e0733f7d/handoffs/2026-04-09T23-19-18-248Z__fast-surfaces-dashboard-view-level-error-and-a11y-contract-tests__1e863ae7-732e-448a-9aad-cf531219225d.json:14,26,100).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix feature handoff commit does not contain feature implementation changes, so reviewability depends on older commits outside the declared fix commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature handoff commit is `38b3ada9e8ddae55b6ac71457682bf8dc187a1af`; `git show --stat` for that commit lists only `.factory/validation/...` review/synthesis files and no DashboardQuickSwitchView or SwitcherDashboardUITests source changes.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the fix-session transcript skeleton, the declared fix commit diff, and the original/fix diffs tied to prior failed features. The declared fix commit contains no implementation changes, and blocking assertion-quality gaps identified in prior dashboard error/a11y reviews remain present in current DashboardQuickSwitchView-level tests.
